tab:alpha_118m.caption: DyT α init sweep (64M/118M, 3-seed compute environment completion Apr 2026)
tab:alpha_118m._mechanism_finding: At 64M/118M, increasing alpha weakens activation bounding and monotonically reduces DyT's data-rich penalty: +42.0% (alpha=0.5), +31.4% (alpha=1.0), +18.8% (alpha=2.0 canonical), +15.5% (alpha=3.0).

tab:r5_llama_ablation.caption: §3.X Llama component ablation (118M, 3-seed, Apr 19) — saturation + val_loss joint table
tab:r5_llama_ablation._saturation_source: out/saturation_sweep/saturation_results.json — N_BATCHES=50 forward passes, bf16, GPU H100
tab:r5_llama_ablation._mechanism_finding: Saturation ≥ 0.5 threshold PERFECTLY predicts collapse (4/4 collapse hit, 0/5 false positive). Pearson r(sat, val_loss) = 0.939 (n=9). SwiGLU removal halves saturation (0.45→0.26) → DyT exits tanh-tail regime → all 3 seeds converge tight.

ablate_gqa.note: bimodal: s1337 (sat=0.276 < 0.5) converged at 4.80; s42/s7 (sat>0.7) collapsed ~7.45. High saturation variance drives bimodal outcome.

ablate_swiglu.note: RESCUES DyT: saturation halved (0.45→0.26), ultra-tight across seeds (val std 0.007, sat std 0.002), -20.5% vs DyT+Llama baseline. SwiGLU × DyT = destructive interference confirmed mechanistically.

tab:diff_attn_v2_scale3_control._STATUS: SUPERSEDED by folder-rename tracking — content is valid Scale 3 data (n_layer=24/n_head=16/n_embd=1024, 354M, V2) living in historically mislabeled folder 'diff_attn_v2_scale5/'. Verify-ckpt-configs Layer A would see path-component 'scale5' and expect 32/32/3072; this is known-historical and not a drift regression. Paper §5.3 sigmoid-lambda ablation Scale 3 control cites these values (9.140/2.391) as valid Scale 3 data. Folder renaming deferred to post-arXiv v1 to preserve reproducibility of reported values.
tab:diff_attn_v2_scale3_control.caption: §5.3 sigmoid-lambda ablation Scale 3 control (354M) — reproduces V1 Scale 3 within 0.8pp. NOT Scale 5; original folder name 'diff_attn_v2_scale5' was MISLABELED (launcher comment admitted Scale 3 configs). TRUE Scale 5 V1 + sigmoid-lambda ablation 3-seed data below (tab:diff_attn_v2_scale5_TRUE).
tab:diff_attn_v2_scale3_control._bug_note: Apr 21 2026 audit: all 6 runs in 'diff_attn_v2_scale5/' folder actually used Scale 3 configs (n_layer=24, n_head=16, n_embd=1024, lr=3e-4 → 404M). Paper's -30.3% headline compared sigmoid-lambda ablation@354M vs vanilla@3.78B = invalid. Corrected: sigmoid-lambda ablation Scale 3 vs vanilla Scale 3 (3.355±.018 per EXPERIMENTS.md:279) = -28.7%.

diffattn_v2_scale3_control_1m_3seed.vs_baseline: vs vanilla_scale3_1m (7.842±0.073, note: SAME as Scale 5 vanilla 1M): +16.6% HARMFUL
diffattn_v2_scale3_control_1m_3seed.note: Sigmoid-lambda ablation at Scale 3 data-poor regime — matches DyT harmful-at-1M pattern

diffattn_v2_scale3_control_118m_3seed.vs_baseline: vs vanilla_scale3_118m (3.355±0.018 per EXPERIMENTS.md:279): -28.7% improvement. Matches V1 Scale 3 (-27.9% per tab:scaling) within 0.8pp.
diffattn_v2_scale3_control_118m_3seed.note: Scale 3 sigmoid-lambda ablation control (not Scale 5). Scale 5 V1 + sigmoid-lambda ablation rerun completed separately (original mislabeled run).

tab:diff_attn_v2_scale5_TRUE.caption: Appendix M + tab:scaling TRUE Scale 5 V1 + sigmoid-lambda ablation 3-seed data — H100 NVL, canonical L=32/H=32/E=3072 per ADR 0007 Layer A. V1 / sigmoid-lambda ablation 1M cells are harmful vs matched 1M vanilla; V1 / sigmoid-lambda ablation 118M cells exhibit high-loss collapse. Sigmoid-bounding λ alone does not repair the 118M collapse in our compute budget.
tab:diff_attn_v2_scale5_TRUE._launcher: scripts/diff_attn_v1v2_scale5_TRUE.sh (n_layer=32, n_head=32, n_embd=3072, lr=1e-4, bs=1 ga=64 eff=64)
tab:diff_attn_v2_scale5_TRUE._runs: 12 of 12 DONE as of internal timestamp (DONE_diff_attn_v1v2_scale5.txt marker written; master.log confirms device0_fails=0 device1_fails=0 missing_ckpts=0)

v1_1m_3seed.note: All 3 seeds completed to iter=5000 with finite losses and checkpoints; harmful 1M cell, not OOM/job failure.

v1_118m_3seed.note: All 3 seeds completed to iter=5000 with finite high losses and checkpoints; collapse means severe optimization failure, not OOM/job failure. Pattern is consistent with V1 instability at scale.

v2_1m_3seed.note: All 3 sigmoid-lambda ablation seeds completed to iter=5000 with finite losses and checkpoints; s7 bv=9.39 slightly better than s1337. Harmful 1M cell, not OOM/job failure.

v2_118m_3seed.vs_baseline: vs vanilla S5 118M (3.431±0.122): +271% HARMFUL (collapse; marginally worse than V1 +207%)
v2_118m_3seed.note: Sigmoid-bounding lambda alone does NOT repair V1-style Scale 5 high-loss collapse; s7 diverges to 17.47 widening std. All seeds completed to iter=5000 with finite losses, so this is a scaling-budget/optimization phenomenon, not OOM/job failure or V1-specific exponential overflow.

tab:diff_attn_v2_scale5_TRUE.paper_claim: DiffAttn V1 and the V2-inspired sigmoid-lambda ablation are harmful at Scale 5/1M (+34.8%/+25.2% vs matched 1M vanilla) and exhibit finite high-loss collapse at Scale 5/118M (+207%/+271% vs matched 118M vanilla) in the 5K-step compute-limited regime. All 12 stress-test jobs completed to iter=5000 with checkpoints; collapse here does not mean OOM/job failure. The sigmoid-bounded λ ablation does not resolve the 118M instability at this scale, but this is not a claim about faithful full DIFF V2. Paper Appendix M + tab:scaling report this scoped Scale 5 DiffAttn finding.

tab:effective_rank_and_norm.caption: §Theory: Effective rank + Frobenius norm, n=3 per condition, fixed data batch
tab:effective_rank_and_norm.method: Per-layer Shannon effective rank = exp(H(normalized singular values)). Fro_norm = sqrt(sum of param Frobenius squared).
tab:effective_rank_and_norm.sigma_over_mu: <= 1% for fro_norm, < 0.1% for eff_rank — publication-grade tightness

dyt_vs_vanilla_fro_ratio.trend: monotonic; DyT increases fro_norm at 64M, decreases at 354M

dyt_vs_vanilla_rank_ratio.trend: DyT reduces effective rank at all scales; gap narrows with scale

tab:effective_rank_and_norm.paper_claim: DyT reduces effective rank by 2-5% across scales (n=3, sigma/mu < 0.5%); fro_norm shows scale-dependent transition (+2.9% at 64M, -6.2% at 354M) consistent with DyT-induced shift toward minimum-norm solutions at larger scales.
tab:effective_rank_and_norm.lit_connection: Effective rank ↔ Singhal & Kim NeurIPS 2025 (LayerNorm + memorization). Min-norm bias ↔ Soudry et al. 2018 implicit bias of SGD.
tab:effective_rank_and_norm.replaces: Failed Hessian sharpness attempt (see apr20_hessian_failed_experiment). This is the SOLID theory quantity.

tab:scale25_true_162m.model: n_layer=16 n_head=14 n_embd=896 block_size=512 (TRUE interpolation between Scale 2 124M / 12L/12H/768E and Scale 3 354M / 24L/16H/1024E). Non-embed params ~154M. Total params (incl wte 50304x896 + wpe 1024x896) = 199.24M at training time (confirmed via train.py smoke).

predictor_check.predicted: DyT not helpful (saturation below threshold, no data-rich benefit)
predictor_check.predicted_118m: DyT harmful (data-rich regime, past saturation flip point)
predictor_check.observed_118m: DyT +11.84% (strongly harmful, confirms predictor at TRUE held-out scale point)

comparison_vs_old_wrong_330M.narrative: TRUE 162M 118M result (+11.84%) matches old 330M 118M result (+11.3%) within 0.5pp seed noise. Paper §3.5 held-out narrative preserved. 1M regime shows both near-zero (expected at T/P=0.006), different sign is sub-noise.

tab:activation_eff_rank.caption: Activation effective rank (forward-pass SVD) 3-seed S1-3 x 1M/118M
tab:activation_eff_rank.method: Forward pass -> hook every transformer block output -> reshape (B*T, D) -> SVD -> exp(Shannon entropy of normalized singular values) -> mean across blocks.
tab:activation_eff_rank.paper_claim: DyT reduces activation eff_rank 43-61% across all scales; verifies Apr 10 0.40 ratio claim (actual 0.40-0.57)

tab:lipschitz_full.caption: Output Lipschitz (noise stability) uniform 3-seed S1-3 x 1M/118M
tab:lipschitz_full.method: Perturb token embedding by eps*N(0,I), measure delta(logits)_F / noise_F. eps=0.01, 3 trials/ckpt, B=16 T=512.
tab:lipschitz_full.paper_claim: Sign of DyT Lipschitz gap flips with data budget at all 3 scales: +4-25% at 1M (less smooth under saturation), -4-10% at 118M (more smooth near-linear).

tab:lambada_118m.caption: LAMBADA last-token prediction downstream eval, GPT-2 118M-trained, 500 passages
tab:lambada_118m.method: Wikitext-trained final ckpts -> LAMBADA first-500 passages -> last-token argmax accuracy + per-token PPL.
tab:lambada_118m.paper_claim: DyT val-loss gap (10-20%) translates to 3-22x LAMBADA accuracy gap. DiffAttn wikitext-PPL benefit does NOT transfer to LAMBADA OOD.

tab:lambada_ood_v2_external.caption: LAMBADA independent GPU validation
tab:lambada_ood_v2_external.method: Identical eval protocol as tab:lambada_118m (GPT-2 tokenizer, last-token argmax, wikitext-trained ckpts, 500 LAMBADA test passages). Fresh hardware (high-memory GPU GPU 96GB, rerun) + fresh env (torch 2.8+cu128, chardet 5.2). ADR 0007 Layer A folder↔config assert added at ckpt load.
tab:lambada_ood_v2_external.eval_hw: NVIDIA high-memory GPU GPU Server Edition

apr20_external_restart_incident.mitigation: Relaunched scale25 tmux at internal timestamp with skip-if-exists. Partial 91%/88% runs accepted as data points. 6 dyt variants will run fresh. Abandoned external s7 pair permanently (canonical).
apr20_external_restart_incident.lesson: Shared external dev boxes subject to restarts. For future long runs, use compute environment (institutional uptime) OR establish resume-from-ckpt protocol in train.py (currently --init_from=scratch only).

apr20_hessian_failed_experiment.description: Attempted 3-seed Hessian top-eigenvalue via power iteration. FAILED as publishable.

single_seed_v1_retracted.values: Scale 1 vanilla=71.67 DyT=79.91; Scale 2 vanilla=45.64 DyT=88.00; Scale 3 vanilla=34.71 DyT=37.14
single_seed_v1_retracted.lesson: Single-seed eigenvalue is random artifact of power-iter init vector, NOT signal. Retract initial "DyT +11-93% sharper" claim.

three_seed_v3_actual.failure_mode: Huge variance (σ/μ up to 94%), negative eigenvalues (saddle directions), not reproducible

apr20_hessian_failed_experiment.root_cause: (1) batch=4 too small, (2) power iteration 15 iters converged to different saddle directions per seed, (3) different data batches per seed = different Hessians, (4) flash-attn disabled for double-backward changes numerics
apr20_hessian_failed_experiment.v1_paper_decision: OMIT Hessian subsection. Theory stays "preliminary, pending rigorous sharpness analysis (future work)".

future_work.library: PyHessian (Yao et al. 2020) — pip install pyhessian
future_work.protocol: batch=16-32, Lanczos top-k (not just power iter), trace via Hutchinson, 3 seeds with SAME data batch per scale
